question.title: How to set ANdroid app audio volume programmatically
question.body:
I am trying, unsuccessfully, to set the audio volume of an Android audio player to 100%.

I have an Android app, in Java, that runs on an Android device. The device is a Khadas VIM3L which has an attached tone board, and a touchscreen. The VIM3L is running Android 9.0 (Pie).

I am trying to set the media volume to 100%, or at least something large like 75%. The client is then adding hardware to the output of the tone board. I don’t know all the details.

Anyway, with my current setup (Khadas VIM3L, tone board, touchscreen) the default output seems to be about 25%. So I am trying to change this.

When the app is launched, it displays a trakclist of songs. Clicking on a track, will then play that track. When the track completes, it will start the second track, etc.

First, I tried just using the Android settings – setting the volume to 100% and the media volume to 100%. However, when I restarted the device, the volume is back to about 25%.

I then added some Java code to set the volume to 100%.

Context context = getApplicationContext();
AudioManager audioManager = (AudioManager) context.getSystemService(Context.AUDIO_SERVICE);
int volumeLevel = 100; // Adjust this as needed
audioManager.setStreamVolume(AudioManager.STREAM_MUSIC, volumeLevel, 0);

I have added this code to the main activity on launch. I have also added to the code that runs when the play button is clicked, when the next track is played, etc.

The general observation is this: When I install the app and then run it – the volume is set to 100% for the first track. If I power cycle the device and launch the app again, and play the first track, the volume is at the default 25%. When first track finishes, and the second track starts, the volume is set to 100%.

Here are my observations:

Scenario 1

App incudes Java code to set media volume to 100%

Install the app and launch – volume is at 100%

Close the ap, relaunch – volume is still at 100%

Restart the device, launch the app – volume is at 20%

Scenario 2

Restart the device, touch the volume controls on the touchscreen

A warning is displayed:

Raise volume above recommended level? Listening at high volume for long periods may damage your hearing. CANCEL / OK

Without clicking on anything, launch the app – the volume is at 100%

It seems that the very fact that this warning was displayed, allows the app to set the volume to 100%

Scenario 3

Restart the device, launch the app – volume is at 20%

Play the first track.

When the track ends, and the second track is started, the volume goes to 100%

It seems that setting the volume to 100% can be done at a later stage?

I realise that I haven’t included the whole project here. Also, initially I had included the above Java code just in one place – on the main activity’s OnCreate(). I then kept adding in more and more places, trying to see if it would ever take effect.

If any one has any ideas on what I might be doing wrong / or not doing, or has any questions – all feedback welcome thanks.

answers.body: Your code looks correct and your approach is also somewhat logical, but I see that you have tried this on only one device which limits your debugging. It is difficult to maintain volume level consistently at 100% on all the devices due to several factors like system settings, permissions, audio focus, and device-specific configurations. I would suggest you to try your code with multiple devices and see how they work.
I couldn't manage to do this using Java from within the app. In the end I had to get someone to modify & rebuild the Android OS
